page.content: In a comparative analysis of cytokine levels between subjects with MSD (N=35) and controls (N=25), the following data was obtained. For IL-1β, the subjects exhibited a mean of 0.58 (0.26-0.90) compared to 0.53 (0.26-0.62) in controls, with a crude p-value of 0.22 and an adjusted p-value of 0.21. IL-6 levels were 0.82 (0.15-1.8) for subjects versus 0.50 (0.15-1.1) for controls, yielding a crude p-value of 0.14 and an adjusted p-value of 0.19. In terms of IL-8, subjects had a level of 5.7 (3.8-8.1) while controls had 5.3 (3.9-7.0), with a crude p-value of 0.85 and an adjusted p-value of 0.85. IL-10 showed values of 1.0 (0.84-2.4) in subjects and 0.93 (0.76-1.6) in controls, with a crude p-value of 0.17 and adjusted p-value of 0.13.

lines.text: In a comparative analysis of cytokine levels between subjects with MSD (N=35) and controls (N=25), the following data was
obtained. For IL-1β, the subjects exhibited a mean of 0.58 (0.26-0.90) compared to 0.53 (0.26-0.62) in controls, with a crude p-
value of 0.22 and an adjusted p-value of 0.21. IL-6 levels were 0.82 (0.15-1.8) for subjects versus 0.50 (0.15-1.1) for controls,
yielding a crude p-value of 0.14 and an adjusted p-value of 0.19. In terms of IL-8, subjects had a level of 5.7 (3.8-8.1) while
controls had 5.3 (3.9-7.0), with a crude p-value of 0.85 and an adjusted p-value of 0.85. IL-10 showed values of 1.0 (0.84-2.4)
in subjects and 0.93 (0.76-1.6) in controls, with a crude p-value of 0.17 and adjusted p-value of 0.13.

page.content: Both groups had equal IL-12 levels at 0.26 (0.26-0.26), with a crude p-value of 0.047 and an adjusted p-value of 0.05. MCP-1 levels were 27 (20-40) for subjects and 29 (22-38) for controls, with a crude p-value of 0.38 and adjusted p-value of 0.38. MIP-1α was noted at 0.16 (0.16-2.2) for subjects and 0.16 (0.16-0.1) for controls, with p-values of 0.07 and 0.08 respectively. MIP-1β showed 90 (63-110) in subjects versus 73 (55-88) in controls, with a significant crude p-value of 0.02 and adjusted p-value of 0.02. TNF-α levels were 3.1 (1.8-5.1) in subjects and 3.2 (2.4-3.8) in controls, yielding a crude p-value of 0.81 and adjusted p-value of 0.65. CTGF levels were 244 (67-420) in subjects compared to 67 (67-350) in controls, with crude and adjusted p-values of 0.63 and 0.78 respectively. Lastly, CRP levels were 0.50 (0.50-1.6) for subjects and 0.50 (0.50-0.50) for controls, with a notably low crude p-value of 0.003 and an adjusted p-value of 0.02.
